steps.title: Identify the boundary
steps.content: The sentence contains two independent clauses: Ritchie's team collected arthropods with one method, and Boyer's team gathered them with bug vacuums. The blank must name the first method and punctuate the boundary between those two complete clauses.

steps.title: Place the example phrase
steps.content: The phrase "for instance" belongs with "sweep nets" because sweep nets are an example of a collection method. A correctly sets off that phrase with a comma after "sweep nets" and then uses a semicolon after "for instance" to join the two independent clauses.

steps.title: Reject the other punctuation
steps.content: B puts the semicolon before "for instance," which separates the example phrase from the collection method it explains and leaves the second clause attached awkwardly after the phrase. C uses only a comma between independent clauses. D gives no boundary punctuation between the clauses. The answer is A.
